title: The Sixteenth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## September 8, 2024 | Preacher: Jack Karn

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon falls on the Sixteenth Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time (Year B, Revised Common Lectionary). The readings would likely include Mark 7:24-37 (Jesus healing the Syrophoenician woman's daughter and a deaf man), Proverbs 22:1-2, 8-9, 22-23, and Psalm 125, with themes centered on healing, faith across boundaries, and justice for the vulnerable. The church was in the long stretch of green Ordinary Time after Pentecost, moving toward the final Sundays before Advent in late November.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of its 81st General Convention held in Louisville, Kentucky in late June 2024, where significant legislative decisions on LGBTQ+ inclusion and church governance had been finalized. In the Diocese of Colorado, there was relative stability under Bishop Kym Lucas (installed 2022), though the broader Anglican Communion remained fractured over sexuality and biblical interpretation issues. No major Episcopal leadership transitions or controversies specific to Colorado were occurring in early September 2024.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical late summer weather in early September with temperatures in the mid-80s and low humidity. The Colorado legislature was in recess between sessions; the next session would begin in January 2025. The primary focus locally was on school district reopenings (Denver Public Schools began classes around this time) and preparation for the November 2024 election cycle, which would see competitive statewide races.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

Hurricane Helene was in its development stage in the Atlantic, moving toward what would become a devastating impact on the Southeast in late September. The 2024 presidential campaign was in full swing with Vice President Kamala Harris (Democratic nominee since late August) and former President Donald Trump competing roughly 60 days before Election Day on November 5. The Gaza conflict continued with ongoing humanitarian concerns, and economic news focused on Federal Reserve policy as inflation showed signs of moderating.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Israel-Gaza war was in its eleventh month with humanitarian crisis deepening in southern Gaza. Ukraine continued defending against Russian invasion with the Kursk offensive having surprised Russian forces in early August, now being contested in September. The Paris Olympics had just concluded on August 11, with global attention shifting toward the emerging international news cycle and approaching UN General Assembly session.

CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was in a moment of intense political polarization with the presidential election dominating public discourse; Trump-Harris debate coverage had saturated media in late August. Sports culture was focused on the NFL season beginning (Denver Broncos began their season September 8), college football's opening week, and the US Open tennis tournament (ongoing through early September). There was a general sense of anxiety about the election outcome and fatigue from the intensity of political messaging across nearly all media platforms.
